analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 8 finding(s) from 11 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Complete absence of debug authentication enforcement.
findings.vulnerability_category: DMI read/write and system bus access (SBA) / hart debug CSRs, system bus memory/peripherals / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external debugger via JTAG/DMI Operation: DMI read/write and system bus access (SBA) Object: hart debug CSRs, system bus memory/peripherals Expected guard: DMI transactions gated by successful jtag_key comparison Observed behavior: No key comparison is performed; jtag_key is stored in a `pass` register but never compared against any expected value. DMI requests are unconditionally forwarded to dm_top/dm_csrs. Path: dmi_jtag.sv: `pass` register assigned on rst_ni, but no usage in enable logic. dmi_req_valid_o is generated solely from DMI FSM state, no gating by `pass_chk` or similar. In dm_csrs.sv line 171, `dmstatus.authenticated` is hardwired to 1'b1. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Attacker can enable full debug (halt, CSR access, SBA memory read/write) without knowing the key. This defeats the entire debug authentication intent.

findings.summary: Secret key storage is writable without restriction.
findings.vulnerability_category: write to ROM2 address range / pkey storage in ROM2 / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: any bus master (CPU, debug SBA) Operation: write to ROM2 address range Object: pkey storage in ROM2 Expected guard: ROM2 write port permanently disabled or address decode restricts writes Observed behavior: rom2.sv uses `we_i` to conditionally write `wdata_i` into `secure_reg`. In ariane_peripherals.sv, `rom2_we` is connected to an AXI-to-memory adapter that passes bus write commands. No write protection exists. Path: AXI bus -> ariane_peripherals i_axi2rom2 -> rom2_we -> rom2.sv we_i -> `secure_reg[addr] <= wdata_i` Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An attacker (including debugger via SBA) can overwrite the jtag_key and other secret keys stored in ROM2, breaking the only credential for debug access.

findings.summary: Debug mode unconditionally elevates privilege to M-mode, granting write access to all M-mode CSRs without separate checks.
findings.vulnerability_category: write to protected CSRs while hart is in debug mode / security-critical CSRs: debug_mode, pmpcfg, satp, mstatus.MPRV, etc. / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debug module (via DMI) once authenticated Operation: write to protected CSRs while hart is in debug mode Object: security-critical CSRs: debug_mode, pmpcfg, satp, mstatus.MPRV, etc. Expected guard: Debug mode writes respect lock bits or require extra authorization Observed behavior: In csr_regfile.sv, `priv_lvl_o` is forced to `PRIV_LVL_M` when `debug_mode_q` is high (line 938). The privilege check uses `priv_lvl_o`, so all machine-mode CSRs become writable. No additional locks are checked. Path: debug_mode_q -> priv_lvl_o = M; then CSR write privilege check passes for any M-mode CSR Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If debug mode is entered (via unauthenticated DMI), attacker can modify PMP, satp, etc., gaining full system control.

findings.summary: In dmi_jtag_tap.sv, testmode_i selects between inverted and non-inverted TCK via `pulp_clock_mux2`. It does not affect state machines or enable bypass. In other modules, testmode_i is passed through but not observed to gate authentication.
findings.vulnerability_category: scan out registers, bypass JTAG key comparison / jtag_key register, authentication state, internal CSRs / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: test/scan controller via testmode_i signal Operation: scan out registers, bypass JTAG key comparison Object: jtag_key register, authentication state, internal CSRs Expected guard: testmode_i de-asserted or isolated from sensitive logic; scan chains exclude key material Observed behavior: In dmi_jtag_tap.sv, testmode_i selects between inverted and non-inverted TCK via `pulp_clock_mux2`. It does not affect state machines or enable bypass. In other modules, testmode_i is passed through but not observed to gate authentication. Path: testmode_i -> i_dft_tck_mux.sel -> clock selection only Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: No direct security bypass observed, but full scan chain integration not visible.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Missing visibility into full DFT scan insertion and whether key register is excluded.

findings.summary: SBA bus master is unauthenticated and can access secret key storage.
findings.vulnerability_category: memory read/write to ROM2 address or other sensitive regions / ROM2 key storage, secure memory regions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debugger via SBA Operation: memory read/write to ROM2 address or other sensitive regions Object: ROM2 key storage, secure memory regions Expected guard: SBA transactions gated by authentication or address protection Observed behavior: dm_csrs.sv implements SBA control registers (SBCS, SBAddress, SBData). Writing SBAddress via DMI sets sbaddress_o, and the dm_sba module issues AXI reads/writes. No authentication signal gates these operations. The AXI master is visible to the system bus, and ROM2 is addressable in the peripheral region. Path: DMI write to SBAddress -> dm_csrs sbaddress_o -> dm_sba -> AXI bus -> ariane_peripherals rom2 interface Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: With OBL-001 (no authentication), attacker can read the jtag_key from ROM2 via SBA, or write to it.

findings.vulnerability_category: assert halt request to hart / hart execution control (halt, resume) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external debugger Operation: assert halt request to hart Object: hart execution control (halt, resume) Expected guard: debug_req_o forced inactive until authentication Observed behavior: dm_csrs.sv allows writing dmcontrol.haltreq via DMI, which drives haltreq_o. dm_top wires haltreq to dm_mem, which generates debug_req_o. No authentication signal inhibits this path. Path: DMI write to DMControl -> dmcontrol_q.haltreq -> haltreq_o -> dm_top hartsel/haltreq -> debug_req_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Attacker can halt the hart without key, enabling subsequent debug mode entry.

findings.summary: No sticky lock on debug module activation after deactivation.
findings.vulnerability_category: write dmactive=1 via DMI after it was cleared / debug module activation / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external debugger Operation: write dmactive=1 via DMI after it was cleared Object: debug module activation Expected guard: Sticky lock prevents re-activation Observed behavior: dmcontrol_q.dmactive is a single writable bit. There is no dmactive_lock or any persistent lock. Writing 1 to dmactive via DMI sets it. After a clear, it can be set again. Path: DMI write to DMControl -> dmcontrol_d.dmactive -> dmcontrol_q.dmactive Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A secure boot sequence that disables debug can be undone by an attacker re-enabling debug.

findings.summary: Performance counters are accessible to user mode without counteren enforcement.
findings.vulnerability_category: read performance counters via CSR instructions / performance counter registers (cycle, instret, etc.) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: user-mode software Operation: read performance counters via CSR instructions Object: performance counter registers (cycle, instret, etc.) Expected guard: mcounteren CSR bits restrict access or counters disabled for U-mode Observed behavior: CSR read process does not check mcounteren; reading CSR_MCYCLE, CSR_MINSTRET returns value without privilege restriction. The privilege check passes because these CSRs have U-mode privilege level (implicitly). mcounteren is hardwired to 64'b0, and scounteren also 64'b0, but that means counteren bits are all zero, which should disable user-mode access. However, the hardware does not enforce this; reads still succeed because the privilege check only ensures priv_lvl_o >= priv_lvl (which is U, so always passes). Path: csr_read_process -> unique case -> CSR_MCYCLE: csr_rdata = cycle_q; no counteren check Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: User-mode can read cycle/instret counters, potentially enabling side-channel attacks.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
